summary: Re-ran the 32-test refactor regression suite against live Railway after the 1-line import fix (added `listVintageLogEntries` to imports in server/routers.ts). Result: 32/32 PASS (11.37s). The previously failing `test_dashboard_getStats` now passes. Verified the 4 smoke-check endpoints return expected payloads: dashboard.getStats (totalTanks=10, activeFermentCount=5, totalLogEntries=32, tankSummaries=10), vintageLog.alerts (5 alerts incl. Tank 8 stuck ferment HIGH and Tank 9 DAP HIGH), knowledge.listSops (38 SOPs), wbsAdmin.listDomains (83 domains), tutor.ask Tank 9/Tank 7 query references covered in test_tutor_references_tank9 + test_tutor_ask_learning_loop (both PASS).
critical_code_review_comments: 1-line import fix is correctly applied — `listVintageLogEntries` resolves and dashboard.getStats now returns the full stats payload with all expected keys.
Suggest adding a CI lint rule (e.g., ts-prune / no-undef) so future refactors of routers.ts catch missing imports before deploy.
test_credentials: Auth bypass on per request; no credentials required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: routers.ts refactor regression is fully green on live Railway (https://ownology-production.up.railway.app). The regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_regression.py is the canonical re-run command: `pytest backend/tests/test_refactor_regression.py -v`. Note: tRPC responses are wrapped as result.data.json — unwrap two levels when curl-debugging.
rca of the issue: Root cause from iteration 3: during routers.ts refactor, `listVintageLogEntries` (exported from server/db.ts) was accidentally dropped from the import block at the top of server/routers.ts, causing dashboard.getStats to throw ReferenceError when computing totalLogEntries. Fix: re-added the symbol to the import statement. Verified by re-running the full regression suite (32/32 PASS) and a direct curl to /api/trpc/dashboard.getStats which now returns totalTanks=10, activeFermentCount=5, totalLogEntries=32, tankSummaries (10 entries) — matches main agent's local values exactly.
